item.title: Trump's proposed arch needs structural revisions to earn final approval
item.description: Washington, D.C., has height restrictions, and the National Planning Commission offered a solution to meet them for the 250-foot proposed arch.

item.title: Charlie Kirk murder suspect said "he wishes he hadn't done it," roommate says
item.description: Lance Twiggs said in a recorded interview with a Utah prosecutor that he saw Robinson the day after Charlie Kirk was shot and killed.

item.title: Charlie Kirk murder suspect allegedly told roommate he "wishes he hadn't done it," interview reveals
item.description: The suspect in Charlie Kirk's murder allegedly told his roommate he "wishes he hadn't done it," an interview played in court on Thursday revealed. CBS News' Anna Schecter has the latest.

item.title: Graham Platner suspends Maine Senate campaign after sexual assault allegation
item.description: Maine Democratic Senate candidate Graham Platner is suspending his campaign against GOP Sen. Susan Collins, after a woman accused him of sexually assaulting her five years ago.

item.title: Writer argues the age of reading has ended
item.description: Gambling is now a more popular leisure activity than reading and emerging tech has worsened diminishing attention spans, writer Rose Horowitch notes in her August cover story for The Atlantic. As less Americans read books and overall literacy declines, Horowitch says that a historic and unique era of reading has come to a close.

item.title: Olympian David Hearn pleads not guilty to charges of damaging Reflecting Pool
item.description: Three-time Olympian David Hearn was indicted July 2, after he was accused of ripping out a portion of the sealant from the bottom of the Reflecting Pool on June 19.

item.title: Home prices across the U.S. hit an all-time high
item.description: Sky-high housing prices make even starter homes too expensive for most Americans, housing data show.

item.title: Millions of Americans have until July 10 to claim refunds from the IRS
item.description: A federal court ruling enabled some taxpayers to seek refunds tied to COVID-era filing deadlines. But that window expires July 10 — here's what to know.

item.title: Schiff launches inquiry into DOJ's closure of tainted baby formula probe
item.description: Sen. Adam Schiff is launching a congressional inquiry into why the Justice Department shuttered a long-running criminal investigation into bacteria-tainted infant formula.

item.title: How Democrats could pick a new Maine Senate nominee to replace Graham Platner
item.description: Maine Democrats are facing a ticking clock to find a new candidate to go up against GOP Sen. Susan Collins this fall, after Graham Platner dropped out of the race following a sexual assault allegation.

item.title: How much interest will a $100,000 3-month CD account earn by October?
item.description: A CD account may feel unconventional for your $100,000, but the short-term benefits will be valuable and multiple.

item.title: Olympian David Hearn pleads not guilty to damaging Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool
item.description: U.S. Olympian David Hearn on Thursday pleaded not guilty to damaging the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool. Shanelle Kaul reports.

item.title: Details on preliminary hearing in Charlie Kirk murder case
item.description: A critical hearing is continuing this week for Tyler Robinson, the man accused of killing Charlie Kirk. CBS News' Anna Schecter reports.

item.title: Questions arise over new Air Force One after Trump uses old plane
item.description: The Secret Service advised that President Trump take the old plane to depart Turkey, according to people briefed on the situation.

item.title: Jon Batiste performs "Georgia On My Mind" at The Great American Block Party 250
item.description: Jon Batiste performs the Ray Charles classic, "Georgia On My Mind," as America rings in its 250th birthday.

item.title: Gold's price is down by more than 25%. Here's what investors should do next.
item.description: The price of gold is down considerably from its record high. Here are three things investors should do in response.

item.title: These are the hidden fees in HVAC contracts (and how to spot them before you sign).
item.description: There are sneaky contract fees that can quickly inflate your HVAC bill. Here's what to watch for before you commit.

item.title: Some large ICE facilities haven't been inspected in over a year
item.description: Detention facilities are now inspected every year or every other year rather than twice a year, a CBS News analysis of inspection reports found.

item.title: 15 ICE detention facilities went without inspections for over a year
item.description: Fifteen ICE facilities holding 500 or more detainees have not been inspected in over 12 months as of late June following a policy change that reduced inspection requirements, a CBS News analysis of inspection reports found. CBS News data journalist Julia Ingram examines what this means.

item.title: Iran, U.S. ramp up tit-for-tat strikes ahead of slain leader's burial
item.description: Iran's former Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei is being buried amid an escalating exchange of fire on the 132nd day of the war.

item.title: Jet fuel prices have dropped. Why are air fares still so high?
item.description: Lower jet fuel costs spell relief for airlines. But that doesn't mean they'll pass savings onto customers by lowering airfares, experts said.

item.title: Here's how much interest a $12,000 high-yield savings account can earn now
item.description: Have $12,000 in a traditional savings account? Here's how much interest you'd earn with a high-yield account instead.

item.title: Prosecutors to play interview with Tyler Robinson's roommate in Charlie Kirk trial
item.description: Video of an interview with the roommate of Tyler Robinson, the suspect in the killing of Charlie Kirk, is set to be played in court Thursday. Robinson allegedly confessed to killing Kirk in a conversation with the roommate. CBS News correspondent Carter Evans reports.

item.title: U.S. star Christian Pulisic fractured leg in World Cup loss to Belgium
item.description: Christian Pulisic was diagnosed with a fractured right leg, the U.S. Soccer Federation announced Thursday.

item.title: Inside Trump's recent dealings with Iran, NATO, Ukraine
item.description: President Trump wrapped the NATO summit in Turkey on a positive note Wednesday, appearing more favorable towards Ukraine than usual. CBS News contributor Niall Ferguson joins to discuss the summit, Mr. Trump's latest actions in Iran and the Ukraine-Russia war.

item.title: North Texas aviation pioneer Wally Funk dies, aged 87
item.description: Wally Funk was selected in 1961 as one of the elite women in the Mercury 13 program, finishing the same rigorous training program given to the Mercury 7 astronauts.

item.title: Iran and U.S. have entered a "new phase" of war, H.R. McMaster says
item.description: The U.S. and Iran renewed strikes despite President Trump's claim that Tehran wants to make a deal. Retired Lt. Gen. H.R. McMaster joins to discuss the current state of the Iran war.

item.title: Stocks slide, oil surges after Trump says Iran ceasefire is over
item.description: Oil prices jumped 6% while U.S. stocks fell as renewed Middle East conflict threatens crude shipments through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Passage: In memoriam
item.description: "Sunday Morning" remembers some of the notable figures who left us recently, including Victor Willis, frontman of the group The Village People.

item.title: Read full episode transcripts of "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" from 2026
item.description: Browse all of our full episode transcripts from 2026 of "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan."
